data.title: Snowflake Is Maintained at Overweight by Keybanc
ICE Canada Morning Comment: Rally Continues
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Intercontinental Exchange canola futures continued on the upswing Thursday morning as the old crop contracts hover over either side of their resistance levels.

With exports and domestic use of canola ahead of last year's pace, tightening supplies will ration demand. However, there are thoughts in the trade that Statistics Canada may have underestimated the 2024/25 canola harvest by about one million tonnes.

Additional support for the Canadian oilseed was coming from increases in the Chicago soy complex and Malaysian palm oil, while European rapeseed was mixed. Gains in crude oil spilled over to underpin the vegetable oils.

The Canadian dollar was higher Thursday morning, with the loonie at 72.11 U.S. cents compared with Wednesday's close of 71.99.

The markets in Canada and the U.S. will be closed Friday for the Good Friday holiday.

About 16,750 contracts were traded by 9:38 a.m. ET and prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne were:

 
           Price      Change 
May       677.70     up 3.90 
Jul       684.60     up 3.90 
Nov       654.70     up 2.00 
Jan       660.60     up 2.30

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. Being Investigated on Behalf of The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. Investors. Contact Levi & Korsinsky For Details
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 17, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. ("The Greenbrier Companies, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Greenbrier reported its fiscal second quarter 2025 financial results on April 7, 2025. Among other items, Greenbrier reported non-GAAP earnings per share of $1.69, missing consensus estimates by $0.09, and revenues of $762.1 million, missing consensus estimates by $136.43 million and representing a year-over-year decline of 11.7%. Greenbrier also announced its "decision to close a facility in Romania," citing "market conditions and a comprehensive analysis in Europe[.]"

Following this news, Greenbrier's stock price fell over 11% on April 8, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/the-greenbrier-companies-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=143632&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. either via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or by telephone at (212)363-7500.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

SOURCE: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Maximus: One of America's Most Innovative Companies
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / April 17, 2025 / Maximus earns national recognition for cutting-edge technology and customer-focused solutions to meet the mission.

Maximus

At Maximus, innovation is what we do. From robotics process automation to machine learning to AI, we use the latest technologies to ensure we get the right services to the right people at the right time.

That's why we're honored to be named to Fortune's annual list of "America's Most Innovative Companies" again.

Our culture values strong customer focus - anticipating needs, solving problems, and delivering high satisfaction for our government agency partners. This recognition is a testament to our dedication to meeting the mission.

About Maximus

As a leading strategic partner to government, Maximus helps improve the delivery of public services amid complex technology, health, economic, environmental, and social challenges. With a deep understanding of program service delivery, acute insights that achieve operational excellence, and an extensive awareness of the needs of the people being served, our employees advance the critical missions of our partners. Maximus delivers innovative business process management, impactful consulting services, and technology solutions that provide improved outcomes for the public and higher levels of productivity and efficiency of government-sponsored programs. For more information, visit maximus.com

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from MAXIMUS on 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: MAXIMUS

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/maximus

Email: info@3blmedia.com

SOURCE: MAXIMUS

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: CarMax, Inc. Investigation Ongoing: Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Discuss Your Rights - KMX
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 17, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of CarMax, Inc. ("CarMax, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On April 10, 2025, CarMax released its Q4 and full year financial results. The Company missed analyst estimates and admitted it would be "removing the timeframes" it had previously assigned to long-term revenue, unit sales, and market share goals. The Company claimed this decision was driven by "the potential impact o broader macro factors."

Following this news, shares of CarMax fell over 17% on the same day. The investigation focuses on whether the Company issued false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose information pertinent to investors. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/carmax-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=143633&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. either via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or by telephone at (212)363-7500.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. data.title: REG - City of London IT - Issue of Equity
data.description:
RNS Number : 5243F City of London Investment Trust PLC 17 April 2025  

JANUS HENDERSON FUND MANAGEMENT UK LIMITED

THE CITY OF LONDON INVESTMENT TRUST PLC

LEGAL ENTITY IDENTIFIER: 213800F3NOTF47H6AO55

17 April 2025

THE CITY OF LONDON INVESTMENT TRUST PLC

 

Sale of Shares from Treasury

The Company announces the sale of 100,000 ordinary shares of 25p each, from shares held in Treasury. These shares were sold today for cash at a price of 455.55p per share.

Following the above sale, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will continue to be 502,664,868 ordinary shares of 25p each, of which 10,422,762 are held in Treasury and have no voting rights. The number of ordinary shares to be used in the calculation of voting rights is 492,242,106.

The number of voting rights in the Company is now:

a)         32,816,140 in respect of the ordinary shares (one vote for every 15 shares held);

b)         30,198 in respect of the First Preference Stock (one vote for every £10 of stock held); and

c)         29,484 in respect of the Preferred Ordinary Stock (one vote for every £20 of stock held).

Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 32,875,822.

The above figure (32,875,822) may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, The City of London Investment Trust plc under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.  

For further information, please contact:

Job Curtis

data.title: Update: ManpowerGroup Shares Fall After Lower Q1 Earnings, Revenue
data.description:
(Updates with the latest stock movement in the headline and the first paragraph.)

ManpowerGroup shares fell 12.6% in recent Thursday trading after it reported year-over-year declines in Q1 earnings and revenue.

The company reported Q1 net earnings Thursday of $0.12 per diluted share, down from $0.81 a year earlier.

Four analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.52.

Revenue for the quarter ended March 31 was $4.09 billion, down from $4.40 billion a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $3.99 billion.

The company said it expects Q2 earnings of $0.65 to $0.75 per diluted share. Four analysts polled by FactSet expect $1.02.

data.title: Lennox Invests in Dallas-Fort Worth Area to Boost Distribution, Customer Service
data.description:
Lennox International said Thursday it is making significant investments in the Dallas-Fort Worth area to enhance its distribution network and customer service capabilities.

Lennox said it has recently opened a larger regional distribution center near the Dallas Fort Worth International airport and is investing in a new 1.2-million-square-foot national distribution center in Fort Worth area, set to be operational by January 2026.

Lennox has opened a new customer experience and training center at its headquarters in Richardson, Texas, showcasing its latest products and technologies, the company said, adding that it has also upgraded its customer service center to enhance dealer support and service.

data.title: Vietnam's VietJet owes investment fund over $180 million, UK court rules
data.description:
Vietnamese budget airline VietJet owes a leasing firm just over $180 million in a dispute over four planes, London's High Court ruled on Thursday.

FW Aviation, part of London-based investment firm FitzWalter Capital, sued VietJet in 2022 over four Airbus A321 passenger jets, which were previously grounded in Vietnam.

The company had argued that VietJet was liable under the planes' leases, which were terminated for non-payment of rent after air travel was curtailed during the COVID-19 pandemic.

VietJet's lawyers said that FW Aviation had recovered possession of the four planes and argued that making the airline return the planes and pay would amount to a "double penalty".

But the High Court ruled that VietJet owed FW Aviation $181.8 million, the latest ruling in a dispute which has been playing out in courts in London, Hanoi and Singapore.

VietJet was last year granted permission to appeal against an earlier High Court ruling that it was liable to FW Aviation, which will be heard next month.

A VietJet spokesperson said in a statement: "The airline has consistently fulfilled its financial obligations to dozens of banks and aircraft lessors supporting its fleet of hundreds of aircraft.

"The dispute concerning four aircraft is isolated and exceptional."

FitzWalter Capital did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

  • Would be lowest close since April 7, 2025, when it closed at $254.71
  • On pace for largest percent decrease since Dec. 17, 2024, when it fell 10.2%
  • Currently down three of the past four days
  • Currently down two consecutive days; down 8.29% over this period
  • Worst two day stretch since the two days ending Dec. 17, 2024, when it fell 14.68%
  • Down 0.6% month-to-date
  • Up 3.67% year-to-date
  • Down 53.28% from its all-time closing high of $563.00 on Nov. 3, 2022
  • Down 19.03% from 52 weeks ago (April 18, 2024), when it closed at $324.83
  • Down 34.98% from its 52-week closing high of $404.52 on July 30, 2024
  • Up 12.45% from its 52-week closing low of $233.89 on Dec. 17, 2024
  • Traded as low as $256.04; lowest intraday level since April 7, 2025, when it hit $242.22
  • Down 10.35% at today's intraday low; largest intraday percent decrease since Dec. 17, 2024, when it fell as much as 10.54%
  • Third worst performer in the S&P 500 today

All data as of 10:07:47 AM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: US natgas prices hold near 10-week low ahead of federal storage report
data.description:
Key points:
  • U.S. LNG export feedgas on track to hit record high in April
  • U.S. gas output on track to hit record high in April
  • U.S. gas storage about 4% below five-year average

U.S. natural gas futures held near a 10-week low on Thursday as the market waited for direction from a federal report expected to show that energy firms added less gas to storage than usual last week as cool weather kept heating demand for the fuel higher than normal for this time of year.

Gas futures for May delivery on the New York Mercantile Exchange rose 0.2 cents, or 0.1%, to $3.249 per million British thermal units at 9:38 a.m. EDT (1338 GMT). On Wednesday, the contract closed at its lowest since January 31.

That lack of price movement kept the contract in oversold territory for a second day in a row for the first time since January.

Analysts projected utilities added 22 billion cubic feet of gas into storage during the week ended April 11.

That compares with an increase of 46 bcf during the same week last year and a five-year average build of 50 bcf for this time of year.

SUPPLY AND DEMAND

Financial firm LSEG said average gas output in the Lower 48 U.S. states rose to 106.3 billion cubic feet per day so far in April, up from a monthly record of 106.2 bcfd in March.

On a daily basis, however, output was on track to drop to a one-week low of 105.5 bcfd on Thursday as spring pipeline maintenance leaves some gas trapped in production basins, traders and analysts have said.

Looking ahead, analysts said energy firms could cut back on oil drilling in the coming weeks due to the roughly 12% drop in U.S. crude futures so far in April.

The crude price drop was related in part to uncertainty tied to U.S. President Donald Trump's on-again off-again trade tariffs, which could reduce economic growth and oil demand.

Any reduction in oil drilling in shale basins such as the Permian in Texas and New Mexico and the Bakken in North Dakota could boost gas prices by cutting gas output.

Meteorologists projected temperatures in the Lower 48 states would remain mostly warmer than normal through May 2.

With seasonally milder weather coming, LSEG forecast average gas demand in the Lower 48, including exports, will fall from 105.2 bcfd this week to 98.6 bcfd next week. Those forecasts were higher than LSEG's outlook on Wednesday.

The average amount of gas flowing to the eight big LNG export plants operating in the U.S. climbed from a monthly record of 15.8 bcfd in March to 16.2 bcfd so far in April on rising flows to Venture Global's 3.2-bcfd Plaquemines export plant under construction in Louisiana.

The U.S. became the world's biggest LNG supplier in 2023, surpassing Australia and Qatar, as surging global prices fed demand for more exports due in part to supply disruptions and sanctions linked to Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

Gas was trading at a one-week high of around $12 per mmBtu at the Dutch Title Transfer Facility (TTF) (TRNLTTFMc1) benchmark in Europe and a 10-month low of around $12 at the Japan Korea Marker (JKM) (JKMc1) benchmark in Asia.

data.title: REG - Hargreaves Servs PLC - PDMR Dealing
data.description:
RNS Number : 5237F Hargreaves Services PLC 17 April 2025  

Hargreaves Services plc

("Hargreaves" or the "Company")

PDMR Dealing

Hargreaves Services plc (AIM:HSP), a diversified group delivering services to the environmental, infrastructure and property sectors, announces that, on 17 April 2025 that, Rebecca Craigen, the wife of Stephen Craigen (PDMR) sold 2,849 ordinary shares in the capital of the Company ("Ordinary Shares") at 562 pence each. Mrs Craigen immediately purchased 2,842 Ordinary Shares into an ISA at 563 pence each.

Following these transactions, Stephen Craigen's aggregate beneficial holding, including his connected persons' interests, is 20,708 Ordinary Shares representing 0.06% of the total issued share capital of the Company.

Notification and public disclosure of transactions by persons discharging managerial responsibilities and persons closely associated with them.

Cavendish Capital Markets Ltd (Joint Corporate Broker)

Katy Birkin / Hamish Waller - Corporate Finance

Jasper Berry / Tim Redfern - Sales ECM

Tel: 020 7220 0500

About Hargreaves Services plc (www.hsgplc.co.uk)

Hargreaves Services plc is a diversified group delivering services to the environmental, infrastructure and property sectors, supporting key industries within the UK and South East Asia. The Company's three business segments are Services, Hargreaves Land and an investment in a German joint venture, Hargreaves Raw Materials Services GmbH ("HRMS"). Services provides critical support to many core industries including Energy, Environmental, UK Infrastructure and certain manufacturing industries through the provision of materials handling, mechanical and electrical contracting services, logistics and major earthworks. Hargreaves Land is focused on the sustainable development of brownfield sites for both residential and commercial purposes. HRMS trades in specialist commodity markets and owns DK Recycling, a specialist recycler of steel waste material. Hargreaves is headquartered in County Durham and has operational centres across the UK, as well as in Hong Kong and a joint venture in Duisburg, Germany.

data.title: Intel falls after rival TSMC denies joint ventures with any company
data.description:
** Intel shares down 1.7% at $18.91 after rival TSMC says not in talks with other companies about joint ventures after media reports co could take a stake in a JV with INTC

** FT also reports on Wednesday the American chipmaker will need licenses to export some advanced AI processors to Chinese clients

** Nvidia down 1.1%, extending losses after closing almost 7% lower on Wednesday amid broad chip selloff; co said on Tuesday it will face $5.5 bln in charges due to U.S. chip export curbs

** TSMC announces upbeat quarterly earnings, lifting some chip stocks

** U.S.-listed shares of TSMC up 2%, AMD up 0.5%, Qualcomm up 0.9%, Broadcom up 0.3%

** INTC closed more than 3% lower at $19.23 on Wednesday

** As of last close, INTC down 4% YTD

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Announces an Investigation Against Sun Communities, Inc. (SUI) and Encourages Stockholders to Learn More About the Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 17, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Sun Communities, Inc. ("Sun Communities" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Sun Communities securities prior to February 28, 2019, and continue to hold to the present, are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/SUI.

Investigation Details

The investigation concerns whether Sun Communities and certain of its officers and/or directors have engaged in corporate wrongdoing.

What's Next?

If you are aware of any facts relating to this investigation or purchased Sun Communities shares, you can assist this investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/SUI. You can also contact Peretz Bronstein or his client relations manager, Nathan Miller, of Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC:

There is No Cost to You

We represent investors in class actions on a contingency fee basis. That means we will ask the court to reimburse us for out-of-pocket expenses and attorneys' fees, usually a percentage of the total recovery, only if we are successful.

Why Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is a nationally recognized firm that represents investors in securities fraud class actions and shareholder derivative suits. Our firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors nationwide.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, X, Facebook, or Instagram.

Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

SOURCE: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: FBN INTERVIEW WITH JOHN WILLIAMS, PRESIDENT, NEW YORK FED
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

April 17, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

JOHN WILLIAMS, PRESIDENT, NEW YORK FED

FBN INTERVIEW WITH JOHN WILLIAMS, PRESIDENT, NEW YORK FED

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

FBN INTERVIEW WITH JOHN WILLIAMS, PRESIDENT, NEW YORK FED

APRIL 17, 2025

SPEAKERS:

JOHN WILLIAMS, PRESIDENT, NEW YORK FED

EDWARD LAWRENCE, FBN CORRESPONDENT

EDWARD LAWRENCE, FOX BUSINESS CORRESPONDENT: Yes, Cheryl, and arguably the second most important person in the Federal Reserve System, John Williams, thank you for joining us on this. You know, the first question I want to ask you is you look at the market volatility over the past 14 days, but specifically yesterday and today, the futures are down again. Does that give you any pause then for possibly a rate cut again in May?

JOHN WILLIAMS, PRESIDENT, NEW YORK FED: Well, first of all, welcome back to the New York Fed. It's great to have you here. And you know, right now I look at where the economy is, the unemployment rate is 4.2 percent based on the latest data we have, it looks like inflation is running about 2.3 percent and, you know, the economy has — had grew very nicely last year, so the economy is in a very good place.

There's a lot of uncertainty out there about trade policy and other things. We are getting some information about that. But really, from my point of view, I think monetary policy is well positioned. I don't see any need to change the setting of the Fed funds rate anytime soon. And it's really about collecting information, understanding better what's happening in the economy during the rest of this year, understanding kind of how the uncertainty plays out and what it means for achievement of our maximum employment goals. But right now, I think monetary policy is in the right place.

LAWRENCE: But are you worried the Federal Reserve might fall behind in rate cutting cycle?

WILLIAMS: Well, I think that we have to look at all the data. You know, my view is based on what we know about tariffs that have been announced and kind of were expected and their effects on the economy, I think we have a period of higher inflation this year and a slower growth path for this year relative to last year. So that's a combination that you have to think carefully about.

What's the appropriate stance of policy? I obviously don't want to be late or in terms of any decision we make, but right now, I think we got policy in a good place and — and just got to collect that data and understand what's happening.

LAWRENCE: So with the rate cuts, the dot plot, the famed dot plot projected two rate cuts. The markets are now saying or thinking there might be four of 25 basis points. Where are you? Is — has your outlook changed in terms of how many rate cuts should be this year?

WILLIAMS: Well, I think it's really hard to say, because first of all, there's just a lot of uncertainty about how the economy is going to play out this year. I think also, you know, it really depends on how the economy responds, in terms of whatever policies happen, and also how inflation is behaving, and, importantly, how inflation expectations are behaving.

I will — I will put a little bit finer point on what the market expects. It. You look at the Fed Funds Futures, yes, something like three to four rate cuts this year. But if you look at kind of where the modal outlook is from, you know, we use options, prices to do that. You know, market expects one to two rate cuts this year. That hasn't actually changed over the last few months. So I think again, what we're seeing in market pricing is a reflection of the uncertainty about the economic outlook and what's going to happen in inflation.

So my own view is, I think that, you know, again, we're going to have to collect, watch the data, what it means for the outlook and the risk to that outlook. But you know, my basic views on where monetary policy is and should be haven't fundamentally changed.

LAWRENCE: You're talking about inflation, CPI inflation, month over month, actually decreased the first time in three years, a 0.1 percent does. Does that, you know, does that put your mind at ease a little bit, that maybe inflation is coming back down?

WILLIAMS: Well, it is. It's good news from the point of view of the — of the, you know, kind of gradual decline in inflation we've seen over the last year or two. It's a sign that maybe the little bit higher readings we saw in the first few months were outliers, and we're still on a disinflationary trend. So I view that as good news.

Still, we're above 2 percent inflation. We still have a ways to go on that. The bigger picture, of course, that's the rear view mirror. The bigger picture right now is how the various trade and other policies affect the inflation outlook.

LAWRENCE: The Fed Chairman said that he could see a one-time price increase with tariffs coming in. Do you see the one time price increase and then could it come back down?

WILLIAMS: Well, I think that you we will definitely get effects on prices and inflation this year from tariffs. That's what we've seen in the past. I think that's a pretty conventional view on how this will play out. I think the big unanswered question is, to what extent does the inflation remain high, say, in 2026 or even longer? We need to make sure that any one time changes in prices don't pass through into more persistent higher inflation. We need to get inflation back to 2 percent after this period, and do that on a sustained basis.

LAWRENCE: That sounds like a higher for longer in terms of rates?

WILLIAMS: Well, again, what the rate path is will depend on how the economy is behaving, what's happening in terms of our maximum employment, price stability goals. But essentially — it's — it's absolutely essential that we keep inflation expectations anchored through this. That's a lesson from history is something that's really paid dividends in the past, and that's really, quite honestly, where I'm focused.

LAWRENCE: Goldman Sachs raised the chance of a recession to 35 percent. Will we see a recession in the next 12 months?

WILLIAMS: Well, I'm not going to make a prediction around recessions or anything like that, because our objective is max — objective is for maximum employment, price stability. So of course, you know private sector economists, you know, are asked those questions a lot. I'm more focused on, what's the outlook for the economy? What are the some of the risks to that, and how do we get monetary policy positions?

So I definitely have a view that growth this year is going to be significantly slower than last year, probably somewhat below 1 percent GDP growth for the year, and unemployment will come up to about 4.5 to 5 percent so it is a slower economic outlook. That's not a recession. That's just a slower outlook, slower growth than we've seen in the past couple of years.

LAWRENCE: What do you — what are your CEO contacts telling you in terms of uncertainty? Are they still nervous about going forward?

WILLIAMS: No, absolutely. You know, I talk to business leaders, you know, from small businesses, medium businesses and global businesses. Uncertainty, and especially uncertainty around tariffs and trade policy is the number one topic. It's very difficult, if you're a business, to make long term decisions on investment, new products and things like that, when you're not quite sure what the environment will look like.

And for the U.S. economy, which is obviously very connected to the global economy and the global financial system, what I'm hearing is it's people kind of pausing on big business decisions and investment, waiting to see how this plays out, and trying to figure out how to be successful. You know, once we have that clarity.

LAWRENCE: In the last 30 seconds, we have the neutral rate. Has it fallen according to the Fed? Has our star come down?

WILLIAMS: Oh — according to the Fed, I can't speak to that. But right now, my estimates, you know, I do say the neutral rate is still relatively low. If you ask me about some of the changes we've seen in terms of the longer run, growth prospects for the economy and through immigration changes and other changes, I would think, if anything goes over, kind of keeping downward pressure on the neutral rate, my view is it hasn't. I don't have any evidence it's changed.

LAWRENCE: And where do you see this — the federal funds rate landing? Where is the bottom for you?

WILLIAMS: Well, my own view in terms of the — in the long run.

LAWRENCE: Yes.

WILLIAMS: You know, my view is with the 2 percent inflation, which we'll get back to. And then, you know, view of the longer run, the kind of neutral real rate is probably somewhere around three quarters of a percent or some plus or minus that. So we're talking about a Fed funds rate around 3 percent or a little bit less. That's just my view, but it could change.

LAWRENCE: John Williams, thank you very much for your time. I appreciate it.

END
